summary: No true integrity blockers — all 21 proposals reference T0-computable supported fields with no forward/post-T0 snooping, no credential access, and no mass-symbol manipulation; main caution items are live-overlap (5 OI_Price/long entries share channel+side with the user's live BWE_OI_Price_monitor pump long), symbol concentration on the new-listing short, and size-amplification on the cross-channel consensus rule.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: session, global_short_ratio_extreme percentile flag, and liquidity_bucket are all observable at T0; ensure global_short_ratio percentile uses trailing-window distribution not full-sample.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Different channel (pricechange) from the live OI_Price/long bot; no same-symbol same-direction conflict expected.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: weekday, oi_change_pct, premium_bps all available at T0.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Same channel (OI_Price) as live bot but opposite side (short vs long); could create simultaneous opposite positions on the same symbol if Monday OI collapse and a pump-long signal both fire — net flat exposure, but doubles round-trip cost.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: All three fields observable at T0; verify top_trader_position_ratio_low percentile uses trailing-window not whole-sample.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Same channel AND same side as live BWE_OI_Price_monitor pump long bot — concurrent fires would double position size on the same symbol/direction, exceeding the 5–10% per-trade sizing target.
risk_assessments.remediation: Add a runtime de-dup gate against the live OI_Price/long bot's open positions before sizing this archetype's signal in paper-shadow.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: hour_utc, taker_buy_ratio_5m, day_change_pct all T0-observable.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Different channel and opposite side from live bot.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: listing_age_days uses listing timestamp (T0-safe). Confirm oi_pct_top_decile percentile is computed on the new-listing's own short history, not a forward-looking distribution.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Same channel (OI_Price), opposite side from live; minor.
risk_assessments.remediation: Cap to ≤2 concurrent new-listing positions and ≤25% of capital across the listing_age_days≤30 universe; report symbol distribution — if 1–2 tickers drive >50% of triggers, gate stricter.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: marketcap_bucket, premium_bps, basis_rate observable at T0; verify basis_rate poll matches T0 timestamp (not next-poll value).
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Same channel AND same side as live OI_Price/long bot — same de-dup concern as the capitulation_long archetype.
risk_assessments.remediation: Same de-dup gate against the live bot's open positions in paper-shadow.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: All three percentile flags T0-computable; verify _top_decile / _extreme are derived from trailing distributions to prevent in-sample percentile leakage.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Different channel and opposite side from live bot.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: session, oi_ratio_pct, liquidity_bucket are all T0-observable.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Same channel + same side as live OI_Price/long bot — concurrent-position risk.
risk_assessments.remediation: Add live-bot de-dup gate; cap to ≤1 concurrent position per symbol when liquidity_bucket=low to avoid micro-structural single-point exposure.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: hour_utc, oi_pct_top_decile, taker_buy_ratio_5m all T0-observable.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Same channel + same side as live OI_Price/long bot — concurrent-position risk.
risk_assessments.remediation: Add live-bot de-dup gate before sizing in paper-shadow.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: Exit-side; reads funding state at decision tick. No forward/post-exit fields. Quant's unsatisfiable-AND concern is a structural/encoding issue, not a research-integrity (leakage) issue.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Exit kernel, paired with paper trades only.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a (encoding fix is Synthesizer's job, not a risk-integrity block).

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: Exit reads current premium_bps; no forward field.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Exit kernel only.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: Exit kernel; reads percentile flag at decision tick. No future-function field.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Exit kernel only.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: basis_rate observable at T0; band trigger is a current-state read.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Exit kernel only.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: Notes say 'post-entry OI drops 5% or more' — this is T0-safe ONLY if oi_change_pct is measured from entry-time to current decision-tick (rolling). If the loop interprets oi_change_pct as a forward-window delta (e.g. 'next 30min OI drop'), that's post-exit-decision data and would leak. Synthesizer must pin window semantics.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Exit kernel only.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a from risk standpoint, but Synthesizer should explicitly anchor oi_change_pct to (entry_time → current_tick), not any forward window.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: volume_pct_below_p25 must reference a TRAILING distribution (e.g. last 60min before decision tick) and not a full-sample p25 — the latter would be an in-sample percentile leak. Same concern applies to all volume_pct_* flag derivations.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Exit kernel only.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a from risk standpoint; Synthesizer pin the p25 reference window to a trailing causal interval.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: Filter only blocks entries — even if the percentile flags were over-fitted, the failure mode is over-blocking (conservative), not generating false alpha. Still, _top_decile / _extreme flags must be trailing-distribution to be honest.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Filter only.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: marketcap_bucket / liquidity_bucket assignment must be point-in-time (snapshot at T0), not the latest known assignment — using current bucket retrospectively would inject mild survivorship bias.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Filter only.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: funding_abs_extreme percentile flag must derive from a trailing distribution at T0.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Risk-side scaler; if applied to live bot orders later, halving size during extreme funding regimes is conservative — beneficial, not conflicting.
risk_assessments.remediation: n/a

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: 120s consensus window uses two past events at decision tick — T0-safe. taker_buy_ratio_5m and oi_change_pct are both T0-observable.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: 'Amplified' size on cross-channel consensus, when one of the two events is the same OI_Price/long signal the live bot is also acting on, would double-load the position — and the 'amplify' verb in the name suggests >1.0x sizing. At 5–10% baseline × amplifier × concurrent live entry, effective per-symbol exposure could easily breach 20%.
risk_assessments.remediation: Cap the amplify multiplier to ≤1.0x in paper-shadow until effective per-symbol exposure is measured against live-bot positions; explicitly de-dup against live OI_Price/long fires before applying any amplification.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: Ordered Reserved6→pricechange within 180s uses two past events at decision tick — T0-safe. move_pct and premium_bps T0-observable.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Different channel anchor (Reserved6) and short side; opposite side of live OI_Price/long means rare same-symbol opposite-direction collisions but no double-loading.
risk_assessments.remediation: Cap to ≤1 concurrent position per symbol; track Reserved6 source-symbol distribution — if a small set of tickers dominates triggers, the strategy is symbol-fragile.
